data.title: U.S. Officials Advocate for Targeting Crypto Crime Over Developers
data.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche and FBI Director Kash Patel have emphasized the importance of focusing on cryptocurrency-related crimes rather than targeting software developers. According to NS3.AI, Blanche highlighted that developers who are not directly involved in criminal activities should not anticipate investigations or charges. Meanwhile, Patel stated that the FBI is concentrating its efforts on scam centers and preventive measures to combat crypto crime effectively.

data.title: Crypto Billionaire's Treehouse Meeting Sparks Debate
data.description: Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting a recent encounter involving Sergey Brin, co-founder of Google, and his wellness-influencer girlfriend. The meeting took place in a treehouse owned by a crypto billionaire, where they confronted California Governor Gavin Newsom. The discussion reportedly centered around various topics, including technology and wellness. The unique setting of the meeting has drawn attention, sparking conversations about the intersection of tech industry leaders and political figures.

data.title: Venezuela and U.S. Hire Firms for Asset Audit
data.description: On April 28, Jin10 reported that the Central Bank of Venezuela announced in a statement that both Venezuela and the United States have hired professional firms to audit Venezuela's overseas assets. The statement did not specify which firms were involved. According to the bank, this move aims to ensure the impartiality of the audit process. The statement cited the bank's president, Luis Alberto Perez, who reportedly made a statement last Friday, saying, 'Regarding Venezuela's overseas financial resources, the U.S. government has hired an auditing firm, while the Venezuelan government has hired another.' Previously, three informed sources in Venezuela revealed that Deloitte, a U.S. accounting firm, has been hired to audit the Central Bank of Venezuela. The audit will cover various aspects, including foreign exchange auctions and gold operations.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Anthropic's Pre-IPO Instruments Suggest $1 Trillion Valuation
data.description: Anthropic-linked pre-IPO instruments on Jupiter indicate a record $1 trillion valuation for the AI firm, facilitated through SPV-backed exposure. According to NS3.AI, this implied valuation has surged by 733% since October 2025, reflecting significant investor interest and confidence in the company's potential growth and market impact.

data.title: Aave and Allies Work to Restore Liquidity After $10 Billion Run
data.description: Aave's founder, along with a coalition of allied crypto projects, is actively working to restore liquidity to the DeFi lending protocol following a significant $10 billion run. According to NS3.AI, this run was triggered by a security breach at KelpDAO a week earlier. The collaborative effort is focused on enabling users to withdraw funds and unwind their positions effectively.

data.title: Trump Urges ABC to Dismiss Jimmy Kimmel Over Controversial Remarks
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has demanded that Walt Disney Co.'s ABC terminate late-night host Jimmy Kimmel following what he described as a 'despicable call to violence' during an April 23 broadcast. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Trump's strong reaction to the incident. The President's call for action underscores ongoing tensions between political figures and media personalities. The situation has sparked discussions about the boundaries of comedic expression and the responsibilities of media outlets in moderating content. As the debate unfolds, both supporters and critics of Kimmel are voicing their opinions on social media platforms.

data.title: Security Breach at Trump Dinner Raises Concerns
data.description: A gunman breached a security checkpoint at a dinner attended by U.S. President Donald Trump, prompting increased scrutiny on lawmakers and the Secret Service. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the incident's potential to accelerate discussions on proposed structural changes to enhance security measures. The breach has raised questions about the effectiveness of current protocols and the need for improvements to ensure the safety of high-profile events. Lawmakers are expected to address these concerns in upcoming sessions, focusing on the balance between security and accessibility at presidential gatherings.

data.title: Decentralized Finance Faces Largest Coordinated Rescue Effort
data.description:
Decentralized finance is currently experiencing its largest coordinated rescue effort. According to Bloomberg, this initiative is characterized by concerns over moral hazard and ad-hoc coordination. These elements contrast with the sector's original aim to disrupt traditional finance.

The rescue operation highlights the challenges faced by decentralized finance as it navigates its foundational principles and the practicalities of large-scale coordination. The sector, known for its emphasis on decentralization and innovation, is now grappling with the complexities of managing a coordinated response to its current challenges.

data.title: Sen. Tillis Opposes Clarity Act Without Ethics Language
data.description: Senator Thom Tillis has expressed his opposition to the Clarity Act unless the Senate incorporates ethics language into the bill. According to NS3.AI, TD Cowen noted that this demand presents a new challenge for the legislation, following Tillis's previous request to postpone the Senate Banking Committee's markup until May.

data.title: UK Inflation Expectations Decline to 5% in April, Survey Shows
data.description: A recent survey conducted by Citigroup and YouGov indicates that inflation expectations in the United Kingdom for the coming year have decreased to 5% in April, down from 5.4% in March. According to Jin10, this change reflects a shift in consumer sentiment regarding future price increases. The survey results suggest that while inflation remains a concern, expectations have moderated slightly compared to the previous month.

data.title: Lightning Network Pilot Shows Promise for iGaming Transactions
data.description: A recent benchmark report by Voltage highlighted the results of a 30-day pilot program in the iGaming sector, which processed 88.2 BTC through 237,000 Lightning payments. According to NS3.AI, the pilot achieved an average settlement time of 1.86 seconds and recorded a success rate of 99.94%. Voltage suggests that the Lightning Network could significantly reduce payout fees and eliminate chargeback risks for gambling operators.

data.title: Goldman Sachs MD Highlights Small-Cap Stocks' Appeal
data.description: Goldman Sachs Managing Director Greg Tuorto has expressed optimism about small-cap stocks, noting they are approximately 25–30 percent less expensive compared to large-cap stocks. According to NS3.AI, Tuorto advises investors to concentrate on sectors such as AI infrastructure, biotechnology, and defense within the small-cap market.

data.title: Bernstein Projects IREN's AI Cloud Revenue to Hit $3.7 Billion
data.description: Bernstein analysts have projected that IREN's AI cloud division could achieve an annual revenue run rate of $3.7 billion as the company transitions from Bitcoin mining. According to NS3.AI, the analysts highlighted that a five-year capacity agreement with Microsoft and customer prepayments are facilitating the infrastructure development necessary for this growth.

data.title: White House Considers Proposal from Iran
data.description: White House spokesperson Karoline Leavitt announced that the administration is currently reviewing a proposal from Iran. According to NS3.AI, Leavitt mentioned that U.S. President Donald Trump is expected to address the matter in the near future.

data.title: Initia Extends Vesting Cliff for INIT Tokens to April 2027
data.description: Initia has announced an extension of the vesting cliff for INIT tokens allocated to early investors, founders, and the team by one year, moving the date to April 2027. According to NS3.AI, this decision underscores the long-term commitment of its backers and contributors. The team plans to dedicate the upcoming year to app development and initiatives aimed at enhancing the value of INIT.

data.title: Launch: Aven Introduces Bitcoin Visa Card with $1 Million Credit Line
data.description: Aven has unveiled a new Bitcoin Visa Card, offering a credit line backed by bitcoin collateral of up to $1 million. According to NS3.AI, the card provides fixed-rate, fixed-term loans with a duration of up to 10 years at an annual percentage rate (APR) of 7.99%. To secure the credit line, borrowers are required to deposit bitcoin collateral with BitGo.

data.title: MARA Foundation Launches with $100,000 Community Grant Vote
data.description: MARA has introduced the MARA Foundation during the Bitcoin 2026 conference, initiating a $100,000 community grant vote. According to NS3.AI, the foundation aims to support Bitcoin network security, open-source software, self-custody access, public policy, and education. The grant vote includes projects such as SateNet, the 256 Foundation, and Libreria de Satoshi. Voting is open until April 29 at 3:00 PM PST.

data.title: Bitcoin Self-Custody Advocated as Civil Liberty at Bitcoin 2026
data.description: At Bitcoin 2026, Congressman Nick Begich, alongside Joe Kelly and Zach Herbert, emphasized the importance of safeguarding bitcoin self-custody as a civil liberty. According to NS3.AI, Begich presented the Bitcoin Act during the event, advocating for congressional action to ensure protections are established, highlighting that executive orders can be subject to reversal.

data.title: Trump Eases Stance on Prediction Markets Amid Legal Battles
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has moderated his criticism of prediction markets, a sector with a weekly trading volume exceeding $7 billion. According to NS3.AI, Trump expressed uncertainty about his position, acknowledging that some "very smart" individuals support the industry. This shift in tone comes just two days after he labeled the sector as part of a "casino." The comments arise as prediction market platforms face legal challenges regarding whether event contracts are subject to federal Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) regulations or state gambling laws.

data.title: Intel's Rally Faces Potential Pullback Amid High RSI and Rising Put-Call Ratios
data.description: Intel's stock closed at $82.54 on Friday, marking a significant increase after nearly doubling over the past month. According to NS3.AI, traders are now speculating whether this upward momentum can push the stock price to $100 before the end of May. The stock maintains a bull flag pattern above $64.84, but several indicators suggest a potential pullback. The Relative Strength Index (RSI) stands at 80.92, indicating overbought conditions, while thinning trading volume and increasing put-call ratios further highlight the risk of a price correction.
